bull.b: A clean capital structure with zero debt and meaningful cash/marketable securities gives Streamex durable solvency and more than 12 months runway at current burn. This buys time to scale GLDY distribution and execute product launches without immediate refinancing risk, improving strategic optionality.

bull.t: Product Launch With Operational Proof Points
bull.b: A live, auditable product that has paid dividends and shows NAV tracking establishes operational capability and product-market fit foundations. These concrete proof points are durable evidence the tokenized gold product can collect AUM and eventually drive recurring fee revenue as distribution scales.

Tied institutional partners materially improve long-term distribution, liquidity and go-to-market reach. Permissioned secondary trading, market‑making support, and IRA access reduce structural barriers to adoption and create potential recurring revenue via protocol/transfer fees and scaled AUM.

bear.b: Streamex currently generates essentially no GAAP revenue while reporting material and recurring losses. Without sustainable fee-bearing AUM or product revenue, profitability remains structurally challenged and the business must materially scale GLDY and follow-on offerings to reach positive operating leverage.

Persistent negative operating and free cash flow means Streamex depends on external financing until products produce reliable cash receipts. Continued cash burn raises dilution and execution risk if AUM growth lags, and it constrains reinvestment flexibility for marketing, compliance and product enhancements.

bear.t: Elevated Operating Expenses And Equity Dilution Risk
bear.b: A sudden, large fixed-cost base driven by stock‑based compensation and upfront go‑to‑market spending increases long-term breakeven thresholds. Elevated noncash equity pay implies future dilution, pressures returns on equity, and makes margin recovery harder until scale economics materialize from higher AUM.
